title: Classification of Sets in a Metric Space and Baire's Theorem
description: A flowchart showing the hierarchy of set types (nowhere dense, meager, non-meager) and illustrating the conclusion of Baire's Category Theorem.
relationships: A nowhere dense set has closure with empty interior
A countable union of nowhere dense sets is a meager set
Baire's Theorem states a complete metric space is non-meager in itself
A complete metric space cannot be expressed as a countable union of nowhere dense sets
